title: Importance-Weighted CV And Shift-Aware Calibration
summary: Test appears shifted relative to train by year/driver mix.
rationale: Test appears shifted relative to train by year/driver mix. Under covariate shift, ordinary CV can mis-rank models, and calibration can break even when discrimination holds up.
implementation_hint: Estimate a train-vs-test density ratio with a simple classifier or coarse marginals over `Year`, `Race`, `Compound`, `RaceProgress`, and driver novelty indicators. Use those weights in fold scoring/model selection, and calibrate OOF logits with a lightweight weighted calibrator rather than unweighted global calibration. Prefer leave-race or leave-race-year folds.
expected_effect: More robust model selection for the public/private test distribution and fewer leaderboard drops from non-transferable CV wins.
risk: Density ratios can be noisy or unstable if estimated on overly fine-grained categories.
